summary.intent: Fix moltis issue #657 by implementing native filesystem tools (Read, Write, Edit, MultiEdit, Glob, Grep) to replace shell-based exec workarounds, improving model quality, path-level security, and observability.
summary.outcome: Phase 1 MVP fully implemented and committed across three commits (db08bd64, 515dc54a, a54fc317): six native AgentTool impls in crates/tools/src/fs/, wired into gateway and policy system, with 761 passing tests and three P1 follow-up beads resolved (cwd defaulting, MCP collision policy, typed error taxonomy).

repo: AgentTool trait is defined in crates/agents/src/tool_registry.rs:12 with name/description/parameters_schema/execute interface
ExecTool (crates/tools/src/exec.rs:194) is the canonical template for new tools — already wired to ApprovalManager, SandboxRouter, NodeExecProvider, policy.rs
file_io.rs is internal-only (used by send_image/send_document), not an agent-facing tool — do not confuse with fs tools
MCP tools are already namespaced as mcp__{server}__{tool} so no collision risk with native tool names
ToolRegistry::register silently overwrites on duplicate keys — no warning emitted prior to this session's fix
The 'coding' policy profile only allowed ['exec', 'browser', 'memory'] — new tools must be explicitly added or they are silently blocked for users on that profile
moltis_metrics pipeline uses moltis_tool_executions_total / moltis_tool_execution_errors_total — no new custom counters needed
just lint fails due to pre-existing llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA build error unrelated to tools crate — run clippy scoped to specific crate instead
workspace lints deny unused_qualifications — must remove fully-qualified paths where type is already imported
Glob/Grep working directory defaults to process cwd (server launch dir), not workspace root — requires explicit path or workspace_root builder field

code.finding: ExecTool — 1.7K LOC, canonical template for new tools; already wired to ApprovalManager, SandboxRouter, NodeExecProvider, policy.rs
coding profile hardcoded to allow only ['exec', 'browser', 'memory'] — must be extended for any new tool to work under that profile
Tool registration point — new fs tools registered here next to CalcTool
McpToolBridge namespaces all MCP tools as mcp__{server}__{tool}, preventing collision with native tool names

workflow: Reference repo survey order: catalog-only repos (claw-code) establish taxonomy; integration-heavy repos (openclaw) reveal edge-case semantics; shell-based repos (hermes-agent) reveal agent-loop concerns even if their approach is what you're replacing
File typed errors as Ok(Value) with a kind field rather than Err — LLM can branch on structured error kinds; Err should be reserved for unexpected internal failures
Match LLM provider's exact parameter names from day one (file_path, old_string, etc.) to avoid paying normalization cost later (openclaw paid this with CLAUDE_PARAM_GROUPS)
Run cargo fetch --locked before committing when adding workspace deps to ensure Cargo.lock consistency
Audit plan completeness separately from execution completeness — the post-phase-1 audit caught gaps in the original plan (typed errors, MCP collision) that were distinct from implementation bugs
File discovered-from beads during implementation for anything surfaced that wasn't in the original scope
Claiming beads before starting work and closing immediately on commit keeps the tracker accurate

friction: bd dolt pull failed at session start ('remote origin not found') — beads may be out of sync with shared store, no local fix available
just lint unusable due to pre-existing llama-cpp-sys-2 CUDA build failure — required scoping clippy directly to the target crate
ToolRegistry::register silent overwrite behavior required investigation to confirm MCP collision risk was zero before filing the bead
workspace lint deny(unused_qualifications) caused compile failures after typed error refactor requiring a second pass to remove qualified paths
metrics API in the codebase required reading process.rs and exec.rs to understand the correct invocation pattern before applying to new files
Glob/Grep cwd-defaulting-to-process-cwd was a silent correctness bug that only surfaced during post-implementation audit, not during initial design
open_items: moltis-sug: Phase 2 — sandbox bridge (FsBridge trait, tar round-trip for Edit/Write in sandbox, native Read/Glob/Grep bridge)
moltis-8o1: Phase 3 — UX polish (adaptive paging, edit recovery, re-read loop detection, must-read-before-write invariant, session-key plumbing)
moltis-6co: Phase 4 — [tools.fs] config section (allow_paths, deny_paths, binary_policy, gitignore, symlinks, docs, hook payload docs)
moltis-tqh: Read has no total-byte output cap (2000 lines × 2000 chars = up to 4MB per call)
moltis-a2l: No contract test for register_fs_tools through ToolRegistry pipeline
moltis-5dz: No fs-tools Cargo feature flag in crates/cli for opt-out path
moltis-tm8: No _session_key plumbing through fs tools (needed for phase 3 re-read tracker)
moltis-oc4: Unknown whether Write/Edit/MultiEdit need to emit checkpoint events for branch-session system
Phase 5 WASM-isolated fs tools explicitly out of scope but noted on epic for future consideration
Code committed to worktree branch only — not merged to local main, not pushed to remote
Streaming reads for large files: currently loads full file into memory even for small offset+limit requests
No performance benchmark gate for Grep vs ripgrep subprocess on large file trees
Cross-session read-tracker persistence: in-memory tracker resets on resumed sessions, must-read-before-write becomes process-lifetime scoped
